title: CollectionBatchImport
description: Batch import wrapper for multiple collection records under the `collections` key. Each record is fully embedded and validated independently. No referenced entities. Records are imported to a tracking database for status management, not written directly to JSON. A collection-batch file plays ONE of two ROLES (see definitions/README.md > 'Collection file roles'): a LOGICAL collections file (records carry `extensions.full_name`, a named catalog hierarchy) OR a MAPPED collections file (records carry `extensions.full_path`, a storage-mirror tree: one record PER FOLDER, `label` = leaf folder name, `parent` = parent folder's collection_id). Exodus auto-detects the role from the records (full_path => mapped, full_name => logical) or via `--type`. IMPORTANT: a mapped collections file is a set of FOLDER DEFINITIONS, NOT a per-asset junction table — a flat list of `{asset_id, collection_id, label}` rows is NOT a valid mapped collections file. Asset membership is expressed separately on `asset.collections[]` and/or `collection.assets[]`, never as junction rows here.

schema_version.description: Version of the batch import schema (semantic versioning, e.g., '1.0.0').

extensions.description: Extension point for batch-level custom fields or plugin data. Keys SHOULD be prefixed with 'x_' or 'custom_' to avoid collision with future standard fields.

collections.description: Array of fully embedded collection records. Each must conform to the Collection schema.
